WMI (Windows Management Instrumentation)

Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) is a built-in Windows management infrastructure that provides a standardized interface for local and remote system administration. It enables administrators and applications to query system information, manage Windows components, and execute commands or payloads. Remote WMI operates over DCOM/RPC (TCP 135 + dynamic ports) or WinRM (TCP 5985/5986). Although wmic.exe was deprecated and disabled by default starting in Windows 11 (2024), WMI remains fully supported and is primarily accessed through PowerShell (CIM cmdlets) or COM APIs.

Requirements

  • Valid local or domain credentials.
  • Membership in the Administrators group on the target host (Local Administrators). A Domain Admin also satisfies this requirement by default.
  • The Windows Management Instrumentation (Winmgmt) service must be running.
  • Network connectivity to the target.
  • Required ports must be accessible: TCP 135 + dynamic RPC ports (DCOM/RPC), or TCP 5985/5986 when using WMI over WinRM.
  • Firewall rules must allow WMI/DCOM or WinRM traffic.

Resources

MITRE ATT&CK T1047
